records.ety: [F. vipre, L. vipera, probably contr. fr. vivipera; vivus alive + parere to bring forth, because it was believed to be the only serpent that brings forth living young. Cf. Quick, a., Parent, Viviparous, Wivern, Weever.]

defs: Any one of numerous species of Old World venomous snakes belonging to Vipera, Clotho, Daboia, and other genera of the family Viperidae.
quotes: There came a viper out of the heat, and fastened on his hand.
notes: ☞ Among the best-known species are the European adder (Pelias berus), the European asp (Vipera aspis), the African horned viper (Vipera cerastes), and the Indian viper (Daboia Russellii).

defs: A dangerous, treacherous, or malignant person.
quotes: Who committed To such a viper his most sacred trust Of secrecy.

defs: Loosely, any venomous or presumed venomous snake.
